consumer_requirements: Release consumers must fail closed by default.
Release consumers must not infer security from missing proof reports.
Release consumers must not accept UNKNOWN, NOT_MODELED, DISPROVED, stale evidence, or missing solver states for blocking claims.
Release consumers must report blocked-production when any blocking claim is non-proved.
Dashboards may display non-proved states but must label them non-secure for production release.
default_consumer_rule: Only outcome prove may be consumed as secure for a blocking claim; every non-proved blocking claim is non-secure and blocks production.

outcomes.definition: The claim is modeled, the authoritative solver returned a proof-producing success result, required assumptions and evidence are current, and all consumer validation checks pass.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: May treat a blocking claim as secure only after validating model binding, assumptions, reviewed evidence, receipt or signature policy, solver allowlist, and release packet freshness.

outcomes.definition: The solver found a satisfiable counterexample to the claim.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: Reject the release and preserve the counterexample as blocking security evidence.

outcomes.definition: The prover did not establish the claim and did not return a concrete counterexample that is accepted as a disproof.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: Reject blocking claims; do not downgrade unknown to warning or manual approval.

outcomes.definition: The production IR does not model the claim, domain, or facts required to evaluate it.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: Reject blocking claims until the production IR or formal claim scope is updated.

outcomes.definition: A required assumption, source fact, model CID, environment profile, or reviewed evidence item is missing, expired, ownerless, unevidenced, unaccepted, or no longer bound to the release packet.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: Reject blocking claims until evidence is refreshed and accepted by the consumer.

outcomes.definition: A required prover, compiler, runtime, or differential solver dependency is missing or unusable for the release gate.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: Reject proof acceptance until the dependency probe reports no required blockers.

outcomes.definition: The aggregate release decision when any blocking claim is not prove or when a release-packet gate fails closed.
outcomes.required_consumer_action: Block deployment and report a non-secure production decision.

out_of_scope: claims absent from the production SecurityModelIR
optional prover coverage not present in the solver dependency probe
runtime behavior outside collected release-window traces
cryptographic primitive breakage beyond listed assumptions
required_input_artifacts: proof report JSON for every required claim
proof receipt or trusted signature validation output
disproof suite report and counterexample vectors
runtime monitor report for the release window

required_release_claims.rationale: A broadcast withdrawal without request, approval, nonce, reservation, and live wallet checks is a direct funds-loss condition.
Over-reservation means internal available balance can be spent more than once.
Customer liabilities exceeding custody plus pending settlement is a solvency failure.
Crediting deposits before finality exposes the exchange to reorg and false-credit losses.
A frozen wallet that can still request signatures defeats incident response controls.
Delegation that amplifies authority can bypass administrative and custody controls.
Revoked capabilities authorizing later privileged actions breaks emergency revocation.
Missing audit linkage is a release risk; a concrete disproof blocks release, while UNKNOWN or NOT_MODELED requires manual triage.

accepted: every blocking claim outcome is prove
every high-risk claim outcome is prove
required assumptions are owned, evidenced, accepted, and current
proof receipts or trusted signatures validate against the release packet
disproof and runtime monitor gates have no unexplained blockers
blocked: any blocking claim outcome is not prove
any required high-risk claim outcome is not prove
any stale-evidence outcome affects a required claim or release packet artifact
any missing-solver outcome affects required proof acceptance
any proof receipt, model CID, report CID, or environment binding fails validation

scope: Crypto-exchange wallet and exchange production release consumers that evaluate proof reports, proof receipts, assumption evidence, solver dependency probes, runtime monitor evidence, and release packets.
title: Freeze proof-boundary and security decision policy
